subtasks.description: Evaluate emergency access request from u_9981 (senior-engineer) for production database db-payments-primary against access control policies. Determine if the request meets emergency incident criteria, if the user role is authorized, if MFA/audit requirements are met, and if time-bound approval is warranted.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask at mid tier: This is an explicit policy evaluation request requiring assessment against an access control policy registry. The task involves multi-signal reasoning (emergency incident status, role authorization, audit requirements) and conditional approval decision-making, which requires mid-tier capability. No additional subtasks needed—the policy agent evaluates the request and returns an approval/denial decision with rationale. No preliminary data queries are required as the request parameters (user ID, role, resource, incident context) are provided.

output.reason: Senior-engineer u_9981 has a valid active P1 incident ticket (INC-20241215-0042) authorizing access to db-payments-primary under POL-501. POL-502 dual approval is waived due to declared P1 status. Access granted under POL-503 emergency provisions, contingent on audit logging and 24-hour post-incident review.
applied_constraints: Access scoped to incident INC-20241215-0042 only
Session must be fully audit-logged with query capture
MFA re-authentication required at session start
Time-bound access: auto-revoke after 4 hours or incident closure, whichever is sooner
Mandatory post-access review within 24 hours by security and DBA teams
All write operations must be justified in incident ticket
